LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF TODD CARPER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST DEFENDANT MICHAEL GARDINER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. MSJ/MSA IS IMPROPER WHERE THE AMOUNT OF DAMAGES RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. DEPT. OF INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS V. UI VIDEO STORE, INC. (1997) 55 CAL.APP.4TH 1084, 1097. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ON SEVERAL ISSUES INCLUDING WHETHER PLAINTIFF IS THE ALTER EGO OF ELECTRONIC PLASTIC, WHETHER THE CONTRACT WAS BREACHED, WHETHER MIST WAS CONVERTED (AND FOR HOW MUCH) AND WHETHER THERE WAS ANY FRAUD. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) [NOTE: THIS MOTION HEARD WITH MATTER ON DISCOVERY CALENDAR.] JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF TODD CARPER'S MOTION FOR THE COURT TO ORDER TERMINATING SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT GARDINER FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH TWO DISCOVERY ORDERS OF THE COURT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR RECONSIDERATION IS GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT RECONSIDERS AND REVERSES THE PORTION OF ITS 2/9/11 ORDER DEEMING ADMISSIONS ADMITTED. AT THE TIME OF THE HEARING, THE COURT WAS NOT AWARE THAT RESPONSES HAD BEEN SERVED, ALBEIT IMPROPERLY. THESE NEW FACTS WARRANT RECONSIDERATION UNDER CCP1008. THE REMAINDER OF THE 2/9/11 ORDER STANDS, AS WELL AS THE 5/5/11 ORDER. IF DEFENDANT GARDINER HAS NOT DONE SO ALREADY, HE IS ORDERED TO PROPERLY SERVE RESPONSES TO ALL OUTSTANDING DISCOVERY AND PAY THE MONETARY SANCTIONS PREVIOUSLY ORDERED WITHIN 15 DAYS. HOWEVER, THE COURT INDICATED THAT DEFENDANT MAY PAY HALF THE SANCTIONS, $2,500.00, WITHIN 15 DAYS AND THE OTHER $2,500.00 WITHIN 30 DAYS AFTER THAT. PARTIES TO MEET & CONFER ON DOCUMENT REQUESTS. IF CANNOT AGREE, MOTION TO BE BROUGHT. [NOTE: THIS MOTION HEARD WITH MATTER ON LAW & MOTION CALENDAR.] JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

Off Calendar Oct-07-2011 Advanced From Oct-21-11 To Oct-13-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion To Compel The Court To Order Terminating Sanctions Against Deft Gardiner For Failure To Comply With Three Discovery Orders Of The Court, And Pay Monetary Sanctions; Or Alternatively, To Impose Evidentiary And Monetary Sanctions For The Same
Off Calendar Oct-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF TODD CARPER'S 4TH MOTION TO COMPEL, AND MOTION FOR: THE COURT TO ORDER TERMINATING SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT GARDINER FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THREE DISCOVERY ORDERS OF THE COURT, AND PAY MONETARY SANCTIONS; OR ALTERNATIVELY, TO IMPOSE EVIDENTIARY AND MONETARY SANCTIONS FOR THE SAME.

APPEARANCES: Ethan Schatz, Esq. of Weisberg & Miller 296-7070 for pltf & moving party; Patricia D. Douglas, Esq. of Law Office of Patricia D. Douglas 703/759-3586 for deft & opposing party Michael Gardiner (appearing via CourtCall).

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions is denied. Defendant paid the outstanding monetary sanctions. Plaintiff should have met and conferred regarding the responses to the request for admissions. Defendant must serve amended form interrogatory responses by October 24 without any objections. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported

Notice Of Motion To Compel The Court To Order Terminating Sanctions Against Deft Gardiner For Failure To Comply With Three Discovery Orders Of The Court, And Pay Monetary Sanctions; Or Alternatively, To Impose Evidentiary And Monetary Sanctions For The Same
Off Calendar Sep-26-2011 Continued To Oct-21-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 21, 2011. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF TODD CARPER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST DEFENDANT MICHAEL GARDINER.

APPEARANCES: Ethan Schatz, Esq. of Weisberg & Miller 296-7070 for pltf & moving party; Patricia D. Douglas, Esq. of Law Office of Patricia D. Douglas 703/759-3586 for deft & opposing party Michael Gardiner (appearing via CourtCall).

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. MSJ/MSA IS IMPROPER WHERE THE AMOUNT OF DAMAGES RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. DEPT. OF INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS V. UI VIDEO STORE, INC. (1997) 55 CAL.APP.4TH 1084, 1097. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ON SEVERAL ISSUES INCLUDING WHETHER PLAINTIFF IS THE ALTER EGO OF ELECTRONIC PLASTIC, WHETHER THE CONTRACT WAS BREACHED, WHETHER MIST WAS CONVERTED (AND FOR HOW MUCH) AND WHETHER THERE WAS ANY FRAUD. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) [Note: This motion heard with matter on discovery calendar.] (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Order Terminating Sanctions Against Deft For Failure To Comply With Two Discovery Orders Of The Court
Off Calendar Jul-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF TODD CARPER'S MOTION FOR THE COURT TO ORDER TERMINATING SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT GARDINER FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH TWO DISCOVERY ORDERS OF THE COURT.

APPEARANCES: Ethan Schatz, Esq. of Weisberg & Miller 296-7070 for pltf & moving party; Patricia D. Douglas, Esq. of Law Office of Patricia D. Douglas 703/759-3586 for deft & opposing party Michael Gardiner (appearing via CourtCall).

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions is denied. Defendant's request for reconsideration is granted in part. The Court reconsiders and reverses the portion of its 2/9/11 Order deeming admissions admitted. At the time of the hearing, the Court was not aware that responses had been served, albeit improperly. These new facts warrant reconsideration under CCP1008. The remainder of the 2/9/11 Order stands, as well as the 5/5/11 Order.
If defendant Gardiner has not done so already, he is ordered to properly serve responses to all outstanding discovery and pay the monetary sanctions previously ordered within 15 days.
However, the Court indicated that defendant may pay half the sanctions, $2,500.00, within 15 days and the other $2,500.00 within 30 days after that. Parties to meet & confer on document requests. If cannot agree, motion to be brought. [Note: This motion heard with matter on law & motion calendar.] (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MINI MINUTES:
